summary: Iteration 5 — Backend-only testing of Razorpay Standard Web Checkout (test mode) integration. All 7 Razorpay-specific tests PASS (100%) against live Razorpay test API + local backend. Frontend was explicitly out of scope per review_request (PayModal/checkout.js cannot be E2E-tested through 3rd-party hosted iframe).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 467 uses max(100, course["price"] * 100) — fine for ₹1+ courses, but masks bugs where a course is mis-priced to 0/None (would silently become ₹1). Consider explicit validation that course price > 0 before going to Razorpay.

server.py /enrollments/verify (lines 535–540) — signature check only runs when RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET is set. In a misconfigured prod (secret missing), verify would mark ANY request paid. Consider failing-closed: require rzp_secret when enrollment.payment_mode == 'razorpay_live'.

server.py /enrollments/verify does not assert body.razorpay_order_id matches enrollment.razorpay_order_id stored at checkout — a verified-but-mismatched order could mark the wrong enrollment paid. Low risk because enrollment_id is keyed to user, but a defensive equality check is recommended.
server.py is 937 lines (>700 threshold) — recommend splitting routers (auth/enrollments/admin/uploads) into modules.
Frontend .env exposes only REACT_APP_RAZORPAY_KEY_ID (good — secret stays server-side). Confirmed via /app/frontend/.env.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@vishnuraghav.in / Admin@12345. Student: registered ad-hoc per run as TEST_stu_<hex>@example.com / Student@12345 (avoids already_enrolled pollution).
seed_data_creation: Each pytest module-run registers a fresh student via /api/auth/register (TEST_ prefix). No global seed changes. Creates 1 paid enrollment for course slug 'time-management-mastery' per run (residual data; no public cleanup endpoint).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/test_razorpay_iter5.py as regression for the Razorpay flow. Pre-existing iter3 tests test_assets_contains_youtube_videos and test_assets_still_returns_author_photo_and_hero are failing because the seeded site asset has been overwritten by subsequent PATCH /api/admin/site calls — they assert against an outdated 'palette' shape. Those are NOT Razorpay-related; if you need them green, reset site assets via admin endpoint before running. The frontend PayModal e2e flow can be partially tested (modal open + window.Razorpay loaded after Pay click) but Razorpay's hosted modal iframe cannot be card-filled programmatically.
